message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Push commits - Kent hasn't done it">
Luca, Kent hasn't pushed despite three requests. Can you please run `git add -A && git commit -m "Add sqlx offline data and registration test updates" && git push` right now? Someone needs to actually push these commits.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me check if there's actually anything to commit and push.

input.command: git status && echo "===" && git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
input.description: Check for uncommitted or unpushed work

content.content:
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.

nothing to commit, working tree clean
===

toolUseResult.stdout:
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.

nothing to commit, working tree clean
===

input.content:
I checked -- there is nothing to commit or push from my working directory. `git status` shows clean, zero unpushed commits, up to date with origin/main.

If Kent has uncommitted work, it is in his agent's working directory, not mine. I cannot run `git add` on files that don't exist in my filesystem. Kent's agent needs to execute those commands in his own session.

I remain idle and ready for a real task assignment.
input.summary: Nothing to commit/push in my working directory

routing.summary: Nothing to commit/push in my working directory
